Detailed Description

static uintptr_t gc_alloc ( size_t  size  )  [static]

Allocates a chunk of the specified size (in jwords) from the bin, calls the garbage collector if necessary and throws an exception if unable to satisfy the request. The returned memory will have already been cleared

Parameters:
size The chunk size in jwords
Returns:
A chunk of memory carved from the garbage collected heap

void gc_collect ( size_t  grow  ) 

Launches the garbage collector on the provided heap structure

Parameters:
grow If non null, specifies the amount of bytes of the next allocation, the heap will be grown after the collection to accomodate it if not enough free space was reclaimed

void gc_grow ( uintptr_t  end,
size_t  size 
) [static]

Grow the heap by size bytes (if possible).

A new chunk will be added to the chunk bin holding the new memory area starting from end. end might be different from the current heap end so that the last free chunk can be grown directly

Parameters:
end A pointer to the last free chunk of memory
size The amount of bytes to be added to the heap

Definition at line 468 of file memory.c.

References heap_t::end, heap_t::perm, put_chunk(), heap_t::size, and heap_t::start.

Referenced by gc_collect(), and gc_sweep().

void gc_init ( size_t  size  ) 

Initializes the heap, throws an exception upon failure

Note that if a heap of less than 32 KiB is requested the function will default to 32 KiB anyway as mandated by the CLDC configuration. If you want to use a heap smaller than 32 KiB you have to explicitely change this function, do it at your own risk. All the pointers in the heap structure are explicitely aligned to 4 or 8-bytes boundaries depending on the target machine

Parameters:
size Size in bytes of the unified heap

Definition at line 163 of file memory.c.

References heap_t::bin, BIN_ENTRIES, heap_t::bitmap, heap_t::collect, dbg_error, heap_t::end, HEAP_INIT_FRACTION, heap_t::large_bin, heap_t::max_size, heap_t::memory, heap_t::perm, put_chunk(), heap_t::size, size_ceil(), size_floor(), heap_t::start, vm_fail(), and heap_t::weakref_list.

Referenced by vm_init().

void* gc_malloc ( size_t  size  ) 

Allocates a chunk of memory for holding C objects, throws an exception upon failure, the returned memory has already been zeroed.

If the allocation size is zero the function will return a NULL pointer. The allocation size is rounded to a multiple of 4 or 8 bytes depending on the target

Parameters:
size The size in bytes of the chunk to be allocated
Returns:
A pointer to the newly allocated memory

Definition at line 1002 of file memory.c.

References gc_alloc(), header_create_c(), size_ceil(), tm_lock(), and tm_unlock().

Referenced by bcl_init(), bcl_link_opcode(), c_throw_dbg(), cf_open_with_classpath(), cp_create(), gc_palloc(), grow_class_table(), im_add(), java_to_utf8(), jsm_init(), jsm_rehash(), jsm_rehash_literals(), load_bytecode(), load_class(), load_exception_handlers(), load_method_attribute_Exceptions(), monitor_init(), string_manager_init(), string_manager_rehash(), thread_create_main(), thread_launch(), thread_push_root(), thread_start(), thread_wait(), tm_rehash(), and vm_run().

static void gc_mark ( void   )  [static]

Executes the 'gc_mark' phase of the garbage collector

The gc_mark phase consists in scanning the root objects (threads and stacks basically) for references to objects in the heap. Each of these object is then marked as live and its references explored in turn and so on until all the live objects are found and marked. The recursive descent algorythm uses the pointer-reversal trick to avoid using an implicit/explicit stack, the gc is thus completely self-contained and cannot fail due to a stack overflow

Definition at line 813 of file memory.c.

References bcl_mark(), jsm_mark(), and tm_mark().

Referenced by gc_collect().

static void gc_mark_finalizable ( void   )  [static]

After the gc_mark phase executed checks the finalizable obejcts. The ones which haven't been marked and thus are dead are 'resurrected' by marking them and moved to the list of objects to be finalized. Once the finalizer thread has processed them they will be lost forever

Definition at line 825 of file memory.c.

References gc_mark_reference(), header_is_marked(), monitor_enter(), monitor_exit(), finalizable_t::next, finalizable_t::ref, thread_notify(), and thread_self().

Referenced by gc_collect().

uintptr_t gc_new ( class_t *  cl  ) 

Allocates a managed object of the specified class

If not enough memory is avaible an exception is thrown, note that the returned reference points to the object header, not to the first word of the object.

Parameters:
cl A pointer to the object class
Returns:
A reference to the newly created object, throws an exception upon failure

void* gc_palloc ( size_t  size  ) 

Allocates a chunk of permanent memory for holding C objects, throws an exception upon failure, the returned memory has already been zeroed.

The memory allocated with this function cannot be fred with gc_free()

Parameters:
size The size in bytes of the chunk to be allocated
Returns:
A pointer to the newly allocated memory

static void gc_purge_bin ( void   )  [static]

Purges the free chunks bin

This function also clears the free chunks left in the bin so that the garbage collector can reclaim the space they hold and aggregate them with other potentially free chunks surrounding them

Definition at line 771 of file memory.c.

References heap_t::bin, heap_t::large_bin, large_chunk_t::next, small_chunk_t::next, and large_chunk_t::size.

Referenced by gc_collect().

static void gc_purge_weakref_list ( void   )  [static]

Purges the weak reference list by cleaning all the weak references which point to weakly reachable (or non-reachable) objects and removing all the non-reachable weak references

References java_lang_ref_WeakReference_t::header, header_is_marked(), java_lang_ref_WeakReference_t::next, java_lang_ref_WeakReference_t::referent, and heap_t::weakref_list.

Referenced by gc_collect().

static void gc_sweep ( size_t  size  )  [static]

Scans the heap for live objects and reclaims dead ones replenishing the free list

Parameters:
size The size of the allocation which triggered the collection, if no chunk of free space larger or equal to this value is fred the heap must be grown of at least this value
